title: Cyclosorus austerus
intro: Cyclosorus austerus, also known as the Australian sword fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and fever, due to its purported health benefits. Bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus austerus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cyclosorus austerus is commonly used to make teas and infusions, as well as being incorporated into tinctures and capsules.
taxonomy: Cyclosorus austerus is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae. This family consists of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns or thelypterids. The genus Cyclosorus is a type of fern that is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Cyclosorus austerus specifically is native to parts of Asia and is found in various habitats such as forests and grasslands. The classification of Cyclosorus austerus also places it within the order Polypodiales.
uses: Cyclosorus austerus has been traditionally used in medicine in various parts of the world, particularly in Asia, where it is valued for its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant is said to have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. Its leaves are often used in herbal remedies, which are believed to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and its potential benefits for human health.
